description: A 'toloi' carries a message or another communicative object between people or places. The messenger may speak the words, deliver a written letter, or carry a recording. 'milaro' (message) is what is conveyed; a message can also travel without a human messenger.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for unvoiced dental 't' and release into middle-height back rounded 'o'. Touch the tongue to the alveolar ridge for 'l' and hold stressed rounded 'o'. Keep the following high front unrounded 'i' in a separate syllable across the 'o.i' hiatus.
usage_notes: The carried message or object can precede 'kolua' (carry), while sender and recipient use their ordinary relations. 'sepho' (send) centres the dispatch rather than the participant who travels.

semantic_domains.communication: a participant who conveys a message or communicative object
semantic_domains.community: a person connecting separated participants through carried communication
